mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A labor market recovery below the natural rate threshold signals peak cyclical demand, prompting equity investors to price in higher corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and elevating equity valuations across the S&P 500 over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production directly boosts corporate revenue expectations for cyclical sectors, generating immediate positive price momentum in the S&P 500 during periods of active monetary tightening.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month during a tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth expands the aggregate earnings base for public companies, providing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if quarterly GDP growth remains positive while 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a hawkish stance, compressing equity multiples and triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances where inflation accelerates beyond the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment paired with rising total employment confirms structural labor market strength, reducing wage-price spiral fears while sustaining demand, thereby lifting equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined indicator yields a negative information coefficient for 60-day forward returns across two consecutive business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Declining residential investment signals a housing sector downturn that typically precedes broader economic contractions, causing equity investors to de-risk and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns during financial stress episodes.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in more than 65% of triggered instances during a financial stress episode.
